title: 10 best herbal creams for low blood pressure

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with low blood pressure because it is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and increasing blood flow. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce constriction of blood vessels, allowing for a more consistent blood pressure reading. Additionally, the cream's ability to stimulate collagen production helps to improve the overall health of blood vessel walls, further contributing to improved blood pressure regulation.
remedy_properties: Adonixanthin: This antioxidant-rich compound helps to strengthen blood vessel walls, which can improve circulation and increase blood pressure by reducing vascular resistance.
Cyclopropenoids: These compounds have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory effects, helping to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help to alleviate low blood pressure symptoms.

Fatty acids: The fatty acid content in Maidenhair Tree creams, particularly oleic acid, has been found to have a hypotensive effect, meaning it can help to lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessel walls and improving circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to improve blood circulation and increase blood pressure.
Bark: Contains compounds like cinnamic acid and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and vasodilatory effects, helping to relax blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and grind 500g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 1000ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a glass jar, mixing until well combined.
Add 50g of beeswax and 25g of cocoa butter to the mixture, stirring until fully incorporated and melted.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a comfortable consistency for application.
Remove from heat, let cool, and pour into sterilized tubes or containers, allowing them to solidify before use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng creams helps with low blood pressure because they contain adaptogenic properties that help to stimulate blood flow and circulation. The herbal ginseng extracts in these creams work by increasing the production of nitric oxide, which relaxes blood vessels and improves blood pressure regulation. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing ingredients help to strengthen capillary walls, allowing them to function more efficiently. This combination of benefits helps to improve overall circulatory health and support healthy blood pressure levels.
remedy_properties: Cardiovascular protection: Ginseng creams contain compounds like ginsenosides and panaxadiol that help to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation, which can help to increase blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease.

Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng has adaptogenic properties that allow it to help the body respond to stress and promote balance in bodily functions, including blood pressure regulation, which can help to stabilize and normalize blood pressure levels.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in ginseng creams, such as panaxyloline, have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help to increase blood pressure and reduce the risk of hypotension (low blood pressure).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and polysaccharides, which help to stimulate circulation and increase blood pressure.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds that enhance blood flow and alleviate symptoms of hypotension.
remedy_recipe: Grind 10 grams of dried panax ginseng roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the ground ginseng powder with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at 160°f for 5 minutes.
Add 20 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well for an additional 2 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream to a glass jar, label it, and store it in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40°f for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with low blood pressure because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to relax blood vessels and improve circulation. This relaxation of blood vessels allows for increased blood flow to the extremities, which can help to increase blood pressure in individuals suffering from hypotension. Additionally, licorice creams may also help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often associated with low blood pressure. Overall, licorice creams have been shown to be a natural and effective way to help alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to increase blood volume and blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of an enzyme called 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which converts cortisol into a form that can lower blood pressure.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants help to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, both of which can contribute to low blood pressure.

Saponins: Saponins, particularly the triterpenoid glycosides present in licorice root, have been shown to improve blood flow and increase blood pressure by dilating blood vessels and improving vascular tone.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to increase blood flow and lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to treat circulatory problems, including low blood pressure, due to their ability to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder 10 grams, sweet almond oil 50 milliliters, and beeswax 20 grams.
Grind the dried glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground root powder with sweet almond oil in a double boiler to create a smooth paste stirring constantly for 15 minutes.
Add beeswax to the mixture and stir until melted and fully incorporated taking about 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai creams helps with low blood pressure because they contain the root of the Chinese angelica plant, which has been used for centuries to balance the body's energy. The cream's active ingredients work by increasing blood flow and strengthening blood vessels, allowing them to function more efficiently and effectively regulate blood pressure. Regular application can help to alleviate symptoms such as dizziness and fatigue associated with low blood pressure, promoting overall cardiovascular health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Cardiac tonic: Dong Quai's cardiac tonic effects help to increase blood circulation, which can improve blood flow and raise blood pressure in individuals with hypotension (low blood pressure). This property helps to strengthen the heart and improve its ability to pump blood efficiently.

Vasodilator: The vasodilatory properties of Dong Quai creams cause blood vessels to dilate or widen, which increases blood flow and can help to raise blood pressure in individuals with hypotension. By reducing blood vessel constriction, Dong Quai cream may also reduce the risk of cardiovascular complications.

Adaptogenic: Dong Quai's adaptogenic properties help the body to adapt to stress and other factors that can contribute to low blood pressure. This property enables the body to respond more effectively to changes in blood pressure and maintain a healthy balance, rather than experiencing extreme fluctuations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dong quai plant are rich in bioactive compounds that help to improve blood circulation and increase blood pressure.
Barks: The barks of the dong quai plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, helping to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried angelica sinensis roots and organic coconut oil in the ratio of 2:3.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Heat the grated coconut oil in a double boiler until it reaches 60-70°c for 15 minutes.
Combine the powdered angelica sinensis with the heated coconut oil and mix well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at 5-10°c for up to 3 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with low blood pressure because of its ability to increase blood circulation and dilate blood vessels. The antioxidant properties of rosemary also help to reduce inflammation, which can contribute to low blood pressure. Additionally, rosemary's stimulatory effect on the nervous system can help to improve blood flow and regulate blood pressure. By increasing blood flow and reducing inflammation, herbal rosemary creams may help to alleviate symptoms associated with low blood pressure, such as dizziness and fatigue, and promote overall cardiovascular health.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, may help reduce inflammation in blood vessel walls, which can contribute to high blood pressure, and promote healthy circulation.

Antioxidant activity: Rosemary's antioxidants, including camphor and bornyl acetate, help neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and contribute to hypertension, supporting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that help improve circulation, which can aid in regulating blood pressure.
Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and other nutrients that help dilate blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and organic coconut oil in equal proportions by weight.
Combine the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and organic coconut oil in a clean glass jar for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids after 24 hours.
Mix the liquid with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 2:1:0.5 by weight heating gently until melted and smooth.
Pour the creamy mixture into clean glass containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with low blood pressure because they contain compounds that help to regulate hormonal imbalances in the body, which can contribute to hypotension. The cream's active ingredients, such as casticin and actein, have a natural ability to stimulate the adrenal glands, leading to an increase in cortisol production. This can help to counteract the effects of low blood pressure by constricting blood vessels and improving blood circulation, resulting in increased blood pressure. Regular use of chaste tree creams may provide long-term relief from symptoms associated with low blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Chaste Tree cream may help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to various skin issues.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with skin conditions like acne, rosacea, or eczema.

Hormone-regulating effects: Chaste Tree's bioactive compounds may help modulate hormone production and balance, which can be beneficial for menstrual irregularities, breast tenderness, or other hormonal symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are commonly used to create topical creams that help stimulate blood circulation and alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure.
Seeds: Containing a unique blend of flavonoids and phenolic acids, seeds are often utilized to develop creams that promote healthy cardiovascular function and improve overall blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and grind 2 tablespoons of dried vitex agnus-castus herb into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the ground herb with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan and heat over low heat for 5 minutes stirring occasionally.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated then remove from heat.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Once solidified, use a spatula to transfer the vitex agnus-castus cream into an airtight container and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams help with low blood pressure because they stimulate circulation, increasing blood flow to vital organs. The warming properties of ginger also help to dilate blood vessels, allowing for more efficient oxygen delivery and nutrient uptake. Additionally, ginger's natural anti-inflammatory compounds reduce constriction in blood vessels, further supporting healthy blood pressure levels. Regular use of herbal ginger creams can promote a sense of balance and well-being, making them an effective complementary treatment for individuals struggling with low blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Herbal ginger creams contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to relax blood vessels and increase blood flow, helping to alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure such as dizziness and fatigue.
Cardiovascular stimulants: Ginger has been found to stimulate the heart and improve circulation, increasing blood volume and pressure, which can help counteract the effects of low blood pressure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds may also help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to low blood pressure by reducing blood vessel tone and constriction.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stem of the ginger plant is used due to its high concentration of bioactive compounds that help regulate blood pressure and improve circulation.
Roots: Ginger roots are utilized for their ability to increase peripheral resistance and stimulate the heart to pump more efficiently, thus helping to combat low blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots weighing at least 100 grams for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested ginger roots in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator for 6 hours.
Grind the dried ginger into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill with 10 grams of ginger powder per serving.
Combine the ground ginger powder with 20 milliliters of coconut oil and 5 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler, heating for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool, and whip into a smooth cream using an electric mixer for at least 10 minutes until it thickens to desired consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with low blood pressure because of its unique ability to dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow and circulation. The herb's active compound, hyperforin, has a direct effect on the cardiovascular system, relaxing constricted blood vessels and allowing blood to flow more freely. This increased blood flow can help raise blood pressure to a healthy range, reducing symptoms such as dizziness, fatigue, and lightheadedness associated with low blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Hypericum perforatum (St John's Wort) extract: Contains hyperforin, a compound that helps improve cardiovascular circulation and increases blood pressure by dilating blood vessels.
Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort creams can help relax blood vessel walls, leading to increased blood flow and improved blood pressure regulation.

Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort's antioxidant compounds help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to low blood pressure by reducing blood vessel constriction.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoids, which have been shown to help regulate blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Flowers: Contain hyperforin, a compound that has vasodilatory properties, helping to dilate blood vessels and increase blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers by cutting them in early morning when the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade or sun drying methods for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 250 grams of dried hypericum perforatum with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil and heat gently at 60 degrees celsius for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the solids from the liquid and discard the solids.
Pour the resulting infusion into clean glass jars and seal immediately, allowing it to cool before storing in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with low blood pressure because they contain the essential oils of Lavandula angustifolia, which have a calming effect on the body's circulatory system. The relaxing properties of lavender oil help to dilate blood vessels, allowing for improved blood flow and increased oxygenation of vital organs. Anxiolytic: Lavender's calming and anxiolytic effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, both of which are common triggers for low blood pressure, and promote relaxation and overall well-being.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots help to stabilize and regulate blood circulation, making it an effective ingredient in treating low blood pressure.
Buds: The buds contain high levels of flavonoids and volatile oils, which have been shown to improve blood flow and increase blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers for the infusion process taking 30 minutes.
Combine one cup of dried lavender flowers with one quart of boiling water in a heatproof container allowing steeping for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into another container discarding solids and reserving liquid taking 5 minutes.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax pellets with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler until melted then add 2 ounces of lavender-infused liquid stirring well taking 10-15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars leaving headspace and allow to cool and set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes.

Adaptogenic agents: Herbs like ginseng, ashwagandha, and rhodiola rosea have adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress, reducing its negative impact on blood pressure and promoting overall cardiovascular health.

Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce blood vessel constriction and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried green tea leaves camellia sinensis and grind them into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground green tea powder with beeswax and shea butter in a heatproof mixing bowl adding 20 grams of each ingredient.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until it reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Add rosehip oil vitamin e oil and lemongrass essential oil to the mixture adding 10 milliliters of rosehip oil and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into a clean glass jar filling it to the top and storing it in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with low blood pressure because of its unique blend of herbal ingredients that work synergistically to improve circulation and increase blood flow. remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves according to their optimal drying conditions for 14-21 days.
Combine dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in a 2:1 ratio and grind into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix ground sambuscus nigra mixture with coconut oil and beeswax in a 70:15:15 ratio by weight and heat at 160-180°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any sediment and discard solids within 5-7 days.
Pour the filtered cream into sterilized glass jars and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or until opened.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese foxglove creams helps with low blood pressure because they contain herbs such as Scrophularia and Leonurus, which have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to tonify (strengthen) the Blood and Qi. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried rehmannia glutinosa roots weighing approximately 250 grams.
Combine the dried roots with 1000 milliliters of distilled water and soak for eight hours.
Simmer the mixture in a pot over low heat for two hours and strain the liquid.
Mix one part of the filtered liquid with four parts of aloe vera gel and refrigerate overnight.
Whip the refrigerated mixture into a smooth consistency using an electric mixer and transfer to glass jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with low blood pressure because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate circulation, increase blood flow, and enhance overall cardiovascular health. Grind 20 grams of dried dandelion root and 10 grams of dried dandelion leaf into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 5 grams of the powdered herb blend with 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler preparation.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 2 hours at room temperature or overnight in the refrigerator before straining and packaging.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with low blood pressure because of its unique combination of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids. remedy_recipe: Gather organic aloe vera gel, coconut oil, ghee, and essential oils of lavender and peppermint in specified proportions.
Melt one cup of ghee in a double boiler or a heat-resistant glass bowl set over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Add two tablespoons of grated aloe vera gel to the melted ghee, stirring well to combine and allow it to infuse for 15 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix one cup of coconut oil with five drops of lavender essential oil and three drops of peppermint essential oil until well combined.
Gradually pour the infused aloe vera mixture into the coconut oil blend while whipping it with an electric mixer or whisk for 10 minutes to emulsify the cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with low blood pressure because of its natural ability to constrict blood vessels, which in turn increases blood pressure. Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in pot marigold have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory properties, which can help to widen blood vessels and increase blood flow, thereby alleviating symptoms of low blood pressure such as dizziness and lightheadedness.

remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers, collecting enough for 100g of dried petals.
Combine 20g of dried calendula petals with 200ml of olive oil and heat for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Add 50ml of beeswax to the calendula-infused oil, melting in a double boiler over low heat.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for low blood pressure are topical solutions that harness the natural benefits of herbs to alleviate symptoms associated with hypotension. These creams typically combine herbs like ginkgo biloba, green tea, and ashwagandha with soothing ingredients like coconut oil and shea butter. By applying these creams to the skin, individuals with low blood pressure can experience a gentle, sustained increase in circulation and oxygenation, which can help alleviate dizziness, fatigue, and other common symptoms. Examples of herbal creams that may help include products containing ginger, cayenne pepper, or hawthorn berry extracts. By using these creams, individuals can regain energy and confidence, improving their overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat low blood pressure with herbal creams is to focus on herbs that stimulate circulation and improve overall cardiovascular health. Ginkgo biloba and hawthorn are two popular options that have been shown to increase blood flow and reduce symptoms of hypotension. Additionally, ashwagandha and rhodiola rosea help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to low blood pressure. Apply a small amount of herbal cream containing these herbs to the pulse points or wrists twice daily for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to low blood pressure that are treated with herbal creams are conditions characterized by circulatory issues, such as Raynaud's disease and poor circulation. Herbal creams containing ingredients like Ginkgo biloba, Ginger, and Cayenne pepper can help improve blood flow and circulation. Other ailments, including varicose veins, bruising, and swelling, may also benefit from the use of herbal creams rich in anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, such as Arnica and Calendula.
